content: My daughter just called - she's thinking about getting married next spring and wants me to produce the music for her wedding.
That's such an honor! How exciting for both of you. Where is she planning to have the wedding?
At our family's vineyard in Napa. She and her fiancé fell in love with it during our last visit. I haven't been back since my marathon in San Francisco two years ago.
The vineyard sounds perfect for a wedding. You'll get to combine your music career with family time. How's your training going for your next marathon?
I'm actually taking a break from marathons this year. Been focusing more on my piano playing and volunteering at the animal shelter on weekends.
That sounds like a nice balance. How did you get involved with the animal shelter? I remember you mentioning your love for animals.
After my golden retriever Buddy passed away last year, I needed something to fill that void. The shelter work has been really healing, and I've even started fostering senior dogs.
I'm so sorry about Buddy. Fostering senior dogs is such a beautiful way to honor his memory. How many have you fostered so far?
Three so far. The latest is a 12-year-old beagle named Daisy. She's got the sweetest temperament and loves when I practice piano.
That's perfect! Music therapy for dogs is a real thing, you know. She's lucky to have you. What kind of music is your daughter thinking for the wedding?
She wants a mix of classical during the ceremony and then switching to more upbeat stuff for the reception. I'm thinking of bringing in a few of my DJ friends to help out.
